LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF HELEN MICKIEWICZ'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES IS GRANTED, IN PART, AND DENIED, IN PART. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DISCOVERY REQUESTS ARE SUSTAINED, AND WITHIN 30 DAYS, DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES AND PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO CERTAIN LIMITED DISCOVERY. (*SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS.*) DENIED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL A FURTHER RESPONSE TO HER REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS AND FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1. DEFENDANT HAS RESPONDED FULLY TO EACH REQUESTED ADMISSION AND ITS RELATED INTERROGATORY. THE COURT ALSO GRANTED DOCUMENT REQUEST 4 (MEMORANDA) WHICH ARE NOT PRIVILEGED AND CONSTRUCTION REQUEST REGARDING BLUE PRINT. MR. WITTEMAN TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SUZANNE I. ANDRADE, CSR # 10682 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF HELEN MICKIEWICZ'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST-AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL FILE THE PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. THE PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT THE DEFENDANTS "NEGLIGENTLY OWNED, MAINTAINED, MANAGED, AND OPERATED THE PREMISES." THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE AT THIS STAGE OF THE PROCEEDING THAT THE CLAIMS AGAINST OPERA PLAZA LP AND OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOME OWNERS ASSOCIATION "ARISE SOLELY BY REASON OF AN OWNERSHIP INTEREST AS A TENANT IN COMMON IN THE COMMON AREA OF A COMMON INTEREST DEVELOPMENT." (CAL. CIV. CODE SEC. 1365.9.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First-Amended Complaint,
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF HELEN MICKIEWICZ'S Motion For Leave To File First-Amended Complaint is Granted. Plaintiff shall file the proposed Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. The proposed Amended Complaint alleges that the defendants "negligently owned, maintained, managed, and operated the premises." The Court cannot determine at this stage of the proceeding that the claims against Opera Plaza LP and Opera Plaza Residential Parcel Home Owners Association "arise solely by reason of an ownership interest as a tenant in common in the common area of a common interest development." (Cal. Civ. Code sec. 1365.9.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MATTHEW WITTERMAN, TEL. NO. 362-3106 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KATE SVINARICH OF THE DAVIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 278-1416 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
SUZANNE I. ANDRADE OF THE BEHMKE REPORTING SERVICES, TEL. NO. 597-5600, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF HELEN MICKIEWICZ's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses is Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Defendant's objections to the discovery requests are sustained, and within 30 days, Defendant shall serve further verified responses and produce responsive documents to the following limited discovery:
(1) Defendant shall search the minutes of the meetings of the Opera Plaza Master Owners for a period of 10 years prior to the date of the subject accident to locate and produce all entries which in any way relate or refer to the design, maintenance, modification, repair, incidents, or injuries occurring on the inner courtyard stairs of Opera Plaza; or provide a verified response that no such minutes exist;
(2) Defendant shall produce all documents in its possession reflecting or referencing a safety related complaint/concern from any source pertaining to the inner courtyard stairs of Opera Plaza for a period of 30 years prior to the date of the subject accident, or provide a verified response that no such documents exist;
(3) Defendant shall provide the names and addresses of the designer and contractor of the courtyard stairs of Opera Plaza from 30 years ago, if known, and the same for any designer or contractor who modified the courtyard stairs of Opera Plaza;
(4) Defendant shall provide the name and address of the entity/company providing security services in the nature of monitoring the security videotape facilities at Opera Plaza for a period of three years prior to the date of the subject accident.
Denied as to Plaintiff's motion to compel a further response to her Request for Admissions and Form Interrogatory 17.1. Defendant has responded fully to each requested Admission and its related interrogatory. The court also granted document request 4 (Memoranda) which are not privileged and construction request regarding blue print. Mr. Witteman to prepare form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Suzanne I. Andrade, CSR # 10682 =(302/EHG)
